changes: ZeroClaw added two more same-cycle releases, v0.6.3 and v0.6.5, extending its rapid-fire Rust release pattern.
IronClaw shipped v0.23.0 one day after v0.22.0, reinforcing momentum in the security-first lane.
NanoClaw updated its README to say outbound requests now route through OneCLI's Agent Vault so agents never hold raw API keys.
TinyAGI released v0.0.20, while Hermes Agent, OpenClaw, PicoClaw, AstrBot, and HiClaw all continued showing active repo movement without a taxonomy reset.
The monitored set still did not need to change: Hermes remains the canonical tracked repo at https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent, and the current positioning map still holds.
whyItMatters: Today reinforces the two strongest non-core wedges in the market: lighter runtime velocity and stronger security boundaries.
ZeroClaw’s release burst is increasingly part of its identity rather than a one-off spike.
NanoClaw’s Agent Vault framing sharpens the security critique from generic isolation to concrete credential-boundary design.
This is meaningful enough for the daily record and outbound digest, but not enough to redraw the stable strategic read.
